data.body:
PASS-1 review — rubric T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety, overlay):

NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS — no findings. T11 is an overlay that fires only when the plan changes a persisted schema/format in-place or backfills existing data; it is migration-EXECUTION safety. This plan does neither.

Grounded reasoning:
- ADD-ONLY, NO IN-PLACE SCHEMA CHANGE: the deliverable is a NEW reducer-IGNORED REVIEW_RESULT event type ("the reducer skips the type") plus an attestation head-state event. It is "intentionally APPEND-ONLY per review run." No existing event format is altered in place and no large persisted table is re-shaped, so there is no blocking DDL / expand-contract concern to satisfy.
- NO BACKFILL: the plan captures data going FORWARD only ("Capture only; analysis is offline"). There is no migration that reads historical rows and rewrites/populates them, so batching/throttling, resumability/idempotent re-run, dual-write window, and partial-failure rollback (T11 a–e) have no surface to apply to. The append-only, non-idempotent-by-design capture is a deliberate observability choice, not a backfill that risks double-applying.
- FORWARD-COMPAT IS HANDLED ELSEWHERE IN THE RUBRIC: the rollout AC ("preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones," fsck WARNs on newer event types, readers-before-writers) is event-version coexistence, distinct from data-migration execution; it does not pull T11 into scope.
- RETENTION/CLEANUP is compaction/archival of the new sidecar ("reuse rebar's existing event compaction"), not a migration of existing persisted data.

ANTI-FP: T11 is explicitly not-applicable for additive/non-rewriting persistence changes. This is an add-a-new-event-type-going-forward change with no historical data rewrite, so the overlay does not apply.

Affirmations (T11): not-applicable for this additive, append-only, forward-capture-only sidecar — no migration/backfill of existing persisted data is introduced; no findings.
